mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in the federal funds rate above 4.5% signals monetary tightening that raises equity discount rates, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of instances where FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.5%, or the Rank IC drops below 0.03 over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Strong payroll growth above 160,000 jobs signals robust labor market conditions that support corporate earnings, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5d returns in > 50% of instances where PAYEMS exceeds 160,000, or the signal's IC falls below 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent declines in housing starts (HOUST) and consumer spending (RSAFS) indicate weakening demand, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX shows positive 60d returns in > 50% of cases where both HOUST and RSAFS decline, or the interaction effect's p-value exceeds 0.1.

mechanism_narrative: A rebound in industrial production (INDPRO) above 103.5 signals recovering supply chains, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20d returns in > 40% of instances where INDPRO exceeds 103.5, or the signal's Sharpe ratio falls below 0.5.

mechanism_narrative: Persistent inflation above 3.2% (CPIAUCSL) forces central banks to maintain restrictive policy, creating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX shows positive 20d returns in > 55% of instances where CPIAUCSL exceeds 3.2, or the signal's IC drops below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: A synchronized decline in unemployment (UNRATE) and increase in payroll growth (PAYEMS) indicates labor market overheating, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5d returns in > 50% of cases where UNRATE declines and PAYEMS rises, or the interaction effect's p-value exceeds 0.15.

mechanism_narrative: A quarterly GDP contraction (GDPC1) below 23,500 billion signals economic recession, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX shows positive 60d returns in > 45% of instances where GDPC1 falls below 23,500, or the signal's IC drops below 0.04.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained rise in industrial production (INDPRO) above 103.5 combined with declining housing starts (HOUST) indicates mixed supply dynamics, creating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20d returns in > 50% of cases where INDPRO rises and HOUST declines, or the interaction effect's p-value exceeds 0.1.
